by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD
P SREE SUDHA IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 18.06.2026

narrative:
On June 18, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 44.0%, resolving 1,142 cases out of 2,596 scheduled across 45 judges. This performance reflects a moderate decline from the 48.9% hearing rate achieved on June 15, 2026, when 1,192 of 2,439 cases were heard, but marks an improvement over the rates observed earlier in the month, such as 40.1% on June 12 and 41.4% on June 5. 

S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the highest individual caseload, hearing 110 out of 279 scheduled matters (39.4%). Notable high-efficiency performances were registered by V. Srishananda in Hall 21, who heard 68 of 90 scheduled cases (75.6%), and T.M. Nadaf in Hall 39, who achieved an 81.2% clearance rate by resolving 26 of 32 cases. Conversely, the division bench of Anu Sivaraman and Venkatesh Naik T. in Hall 2 did not resolve any of their 86 scheduled matters, while Ashok S. Kinagi in Hall 9 cleared just 17.5% of his 177 scheduled cases.

Procedurally, the Orders stage dominated the docket with 989 scheduled cases, converting at a 48.0% rate (475 heard). In contrast, Final Hearings lagged significantly, clearing only 22.1% (116 of 526) of scheduled matters, which is a decline from the 32.0% conversion rate recorded on June 15. Writ Petitions remained the primary case type, accounting for 1,009 scheduled cases and yielding a 43.2% clearance rate. Criminal Petitions and Miscellaneous First Appeals followed, converting at 45.0% and 46.9% respectively, while Regular Second Appeals showed a robust clearance rate of 60.6% by resolving 83 of 137 scheduled matters.
